description: Default flow templates by framework type. Used when no app-specific flow exists.

concerto_principle.rule: UAB is a concerto — never stick to one input method. For EACH micro-operation, choose the most EFFICIENT method. Efficiency = speed + outcome quality + control precision + cost. This applies to EVERY app, not just specific ones.
concerto_principle.decision: For each micro-operation ask: What is the fastest way to do THIS operation that gives me the best outcome with the most control at the lowest cost? Then use that method. Switch methods freely within a single task — switching is the norm, not the exception.

keyboard_shortcut.when: The operation has a known shortcut or can be typed
keyboard_shortcut.why_efficient: Fastest (instant), most reliable (no coordinate guessing), cheapest (one API call), highest control (deterministic)
keyboard_shortcut.examples: Ctrl+S save, Ctrl+Z undo, Tab switch mode, F3 search, typing text, hotkey combos

ui_automation.when: Need to find or read a specific UI element by name/type, or the app has a rich accessibility tree
ui_automation.why_efficient: Most accurate for element discovery (finds by name not coordinates), reads state without screenshots
ui_automation.examples: Finding a button labeled 'Submit', reading a text field value, checking if a checkbox is checked

drag.when: The operation REQUIRES continuous held-button mouse movement — no keyboard equivalent exists
drag.why_efficient: Only method that can express spatial gestures. Without it, sculpting/painting/drawing is impossible
drag.examples: Sculpt brush strokes, paint strokes, slider adjustments, drag-and-drop, freeform drawing, resizing by handle

scroll.when: Zooming, scrolling content, navigating maps or timelines
scroll.why_efficient: Direct wheel injection — faster and more precise than Page Up/Down keys for spatial navigation
scroll.examples: Zoom in Blender/maps, scroll a document, navigate a timeline

screenshot.when: Need to see what's on screen, verify an action worked, or the app has no accessibility tree
screenshot.why_efficient: Only way to read visual state. Essential verification step after spatial operations
screenshot.examples: Verify sculpt result, read OpenGL UI, check visual layout, confirm action completed

clipboard.when: Need to transfer text reliably, especially into apps that mangle SendKeys input
clipboard.why_efficient: Most reliable text transfer for Electron apps and complex strings (Unicode, special chars)
clipboard.examples: Pasting into chat apps, transferring formatted text, emoji-containing strings

cdp_or_extension.when: Browser or Electron app needs DOM-level interaction or JavaScript execution
cdp_or_extension.why_efficient: Semantic access to page content — can read/modify DOM directly without coordinate guessing
cdp_or_extension.examples: Reading page text, clicking by CSS selector, executing JavaScript, managing cookies

anti_patterns: Sticking to one method for an entire task — every task is a concerto
Using drag/click to navigate menus when a keyboard shortcut exists (slower, less reliable, more expensive)
Avoiding drag when the task genuinely requires spatial input (limiting your capabilities)
Skipping screenshot verification after spatial operations (flying blind)

Electron.rationale: Electron apps have shallow UIA trees. Tab behavior varies per app. Clipboard paste is the most reliable universal method. SendKeys may strip spaces in some Electron apps.

default_plan.note: Win32 apps have rich UIA trees
Find the target element by type/label
Direct SendKeys works on Win32 controls

Win32.rationale: Native Win32 apps expose full UIA element trees. Direct interaction via element IDs is reliable.

default_plan.note: Use Office-specific COM actions (readRange, writeRange, getSheets, etc.)

Office.rationale: Office apps have deep COM automation support. Use the built-in Office actions instead of UI automation.

Qt.rationale: Qt apps have good UIA support. Element-based interaction works reliably.

default_plan.note: See what we are working with

unknown.rationale: Unknown framework — use the safest universal approach. Clipboard paste works with nearly everything.
